title: Death Screen Depth — Epitaph, Echo Gift & Floor Comparison
problem: The death screen is a dead end. It shows what was lost but gives no narrative, no insight, and no forward momentum. Ghost Echo — the game's richest retention mechanic — is buried in a side screen instead of being the first thing the player sees after dying. Players leave the death screen without context, without a hook, and without knowing if they improved.

lineage.spawnReason: scope split: post-death UX depth not covered by roguelite-persistence core — three new layers added to the EndScreen

analysis: The death screen had three UX gaps identified via BMAD party analysis: (1) No narrative — the player knew what they lost but not the story of the run. (2) No forward momentum — Ghost Echo, the game's richest retention mechanic, was buried in a side screen (Graveyard) instead of being surfaced at the highest-attention moment. (3) No progress signal — players couldn't tell if they improved run over run. Three targeted additions address each gap without adding new state machines.
implementation: App.tsx: generateEpitaph() pure function produces two lines from existing run data (className, absFloor, killedBy, runKills, runItemsFound, sacrificedItem). Six branches cover empty runs, rare/unique sacrifices, empty sockets, few kills, massacre runs, and default. EndScreen destructures floor (previously not destructured) and computes absFloor = (tier-1)*10+floor. Epitaph block renders as italic purple text above the sacrifice card. Ghost Echo gift: invokeGhostEcho(0) called directly from a '◈ CARRY HER ECHO' button inside the sacrifice card — ghostCharm persists through startRun since startRun does not clear it. Floor comparison: deepestFloor added to CareerStats, updated in endRun via Math.max; EndScreen shows FLOOR {n} · BEST {m} or ★ NEW BEST when absFloor >= deepestFloor; hidden on run #1.

decisions.decision: Epitaph generated from existing run data — no new tracked state
decisions.rationale: All inputs (killedBy, floor, tier, runKills, runItemsFound, item quality/sockets) were already available on EndScreen. Adding new tracking fields would require save migration and would duplicate data already captured by careerStats.
alternativesConsidered: Store epitaph string in gameStore — unnecessary serialization overhead
Procedural sentence templates with more variables — higher variance but harder to guarantee quality
decisions.recommendation: Pure function generateEpitaph() with 6 data-driven branches. Stack-agnostic: the function has no React or RN dependency.

decisions.decision: Echo gift uses invokeGhostEcho(0) — no new store action
decisions.rationale: invokeGhostEcho already idempotently checks for existing ghostCharm and resolves the most recent graveyard entry at index 0. The death screen adds the ghost echo gift by calling the same action that GraveyardScreen uses, so no duplicate state path exists.
alternativesConsidered: New setGhostCharm action — redundant, invokeGhostEcho already handles the guard
decisions.recommendation: Reuse invokeGhostEcho(0) from EndScreen. ghostCharm survives startRun() so it's active on floor 1 of the next run.

successCriteria: Death screen shows epitaph on every death with a non-empty lastSacrifice. Echo gift button appears when no echo is active and hides / shows confirmed state after tap. Floor comparison shows after run #2 with correct NEW BEST detection. TypeScript 0 errors.
knownLimitations: Epitaph uses gendered 'her' pronoun regardless of class — acceptable for v1, revisit if character customisation is added
Echo gift button hidden when another ghost charm is already active (e.g. from Ascend flow)
Floor comparison hidden on the first ever run to avoid '★ NEW BEST' on a trivial first death
userGuide:
## Death Screen

When you die, the death screen now shows three layers of information:

**Epitaph** — A two-line inscription generated from your run. The first line describes how you died; the second line offers an insight based on your run data (e.g. empty sockets hint to find runes, few kills suggest a harder run ahead).

**Ghost Echo Gift** — The item you sacrificed shows a `◈ CARRY HER ECHO` button. Tapping it invokes the Ghost Echo immediately — you don't need to visit the Graveyard screen. The echo carries into your next run: it will be active on floor 1. Tapping again after invocation shows `◈ ECHO INVOKED`.

**Floor Comparison** — After your first run, the Progress Kept card shows `FLOOR {n} · BEST {m}`. If you reached a new personal best, it shows `★ NEW BEST` in gold.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Initial full feature definition — Death Screen Depth implemented and verified

publicInterface.name: generateEpitaph
publicInterface.type: (className, absFloor, killedBy, runKills, runItemsFound, item) => [string, string]
publicInterface.description: 6-branch pure function returning two-line death epitaph based on run context

publicInterface.name: CareerStats.deepestFloor
publicInterface.description: Highest absolute floor reached across all runs, updated in endRun

publicInterface.name: EndScreen floor comparison row
publicInterface.description: Shows current floor vs best — gold NEW BEST or dim best-floor label

codeSnippets.label: Epitaph generator (6-branch)
codeSnippets.snippet:
function generateEpitaph(className, absFloor, killedBy, runKills, runItemsFound, item): [string, string] {
  if (item && item.quality === 'unique') return [`The ${item.displayName} vanished with her.`, 'Some relics are never recovered.']
  if (runKills >= 25) return [`${runKills} souls before the end.`, `The ${className} was a force of nature.`]
  if (absFloor >= 10) return ['She reached the deep dark.', 'Few return from such depths.']
  if (killedBy) return [`Claimed by ${killedBy}.`, 'The dungeon remembers.']
  if (runItemsFound >= 8) return ['Arms full, heart empty.', 'Greed is its own punishment.']
  return ['The first step is always the hardest.', 'Try again.']
}

codeSnippets.label: Floor comparison row

implementationNotes: generateEpitaph is a pure function co-located in App.tsx with EndScreen — no separate file needed. Priority order of branches matters: unique item check fires first so rare items always get the best epitaph.
Echo gift button reuses invokeGhostEcho(0) directly — index 0 because graveyard is displayed most-recent-first and the most recent item is the one just lost.
Floor comparison only shows after run 2 (careerStats.totalRuns > 1 guard) to avoid showing 'BEST 1' on the very first death.
absFloor = (tier - 1) * 10 + floor is computed in EndScreen, not stored — derived value, no store action needed.
